llm_verdict.message:
Implemented PAM password minimum length remediation:

1. Verified `pam_pwquality.so` is used in `/etc/pam.d/system-auth`.
2. Backed up existing config: copied `/etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/99-pwquality.conf` to `.bak`.
3. Ensured `authselect` is present.
4. Removed any existing `minlen` lines (including commented) from all `*.conf` files in `/etc/security/pwquality.conf.d`.
5. Created `/etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/10-minlen.conf` with `minlen = 12`.
6. Ran `authselect apply-changes` to apply PAM changes.
7. Restored SELinux context on `/etc/security/pwquality.conf.d`.

The system now enforces a minimum password length of 12 characters, addressing the OpenSCAP finding **accounts_password_pam_minlen**.
